forecast-period.text: A high pressure system will become established over the Bight during today before moving slowly eastwards during Sunday and Monday. A low pressure trough will move across southern coastal districts on Tuesday then another high will move across waters to the south.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated showers about the Southern Agricultural Area, and a chance of isolated light showers about the south of the pastoral districts and in the west. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southerly winds tending south to southeasterly later.
Isolated light showers mostly about southern coastal districts. Areas of morning fog inland and patchy frost about parts of the Agricultural Area. Maximum temperatures below average. Light winds.

Isolated light showers possible near southern coasts. Areas of morning fog inland and patchy frost about parts of the Agricultural Area. Maximum temperatures below average. Light winds tending west to northwesterly in the south.

text.p: Isolated showers about southern coastal districts, contracting to southern coasts in the evening. Areas of morning fog inland and patchy frost about parts of the Flinders, Mid North and Murray districts. Maximum temperatures below average. West to northwesterly winds in the south turning west west to southwesterly. Southeast to northeasterly winds in the north.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: less than 2 mm about the south of the pastoral districts, 1-5 mm for the Flinders and West Coast districts and the Northern Agricultural Area, 5-15 mm across the Southern Agricultural Area. Isolated totals of 15-25 mm are possible near southern coasts and ranges.
